releases.description:
* Now the user is able to show/hide any children nodes of the marriage separately: #34 
* Refactored the code, so now developers do not need to silence ESLint: #35 
* Added unsaved data detection: now, when the user tries to go to the start-up menu when there is unsaved data, the plugin will ask for confirmation: #36

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: This release includes numerous changes. Finally, after months of work, this plugin is usable. Below is the short list of new features from most to least significant (in my honest opinion). All persons in demo screenshots below are generated using AI. If you find any coincidences with real people, please contact me, and I will fix them. # Visualization algorithms ## Reingold-Tilford It finally works as intended. I rewrote the implementation and added nodes collapsing/expanding, node rearrangement, etc. Demo: This algorithm can render the children's tree as well as the parents' tree. PRs: - [feat: generic Reingold-Tilford tree implementation](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/6) - [feat: implement nodes rearrangement (move left, right, and swap);](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/19) ## Brandes-Köpf A new layout type. It is suitable for family graphs of any complexity. The user can expand/collapse any nodes they want. The nodes are not perfectly centered, but still pretty enough. PRs: - [feat(layout): Brandes-Köpf](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/7) - [feat(layout): Reingold-Tilford: tree manipulations support and refactoring;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/12) - [feat: implement nodes rearrangement (move left, right, and swap);](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/19) # Persistance Now the user can save generated graphs and reopen them in the next session. It allows the user to have a favorite graph collection. PRs: - [feat: implement graph saving and loading;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/11) - [feat: reproducible persistence](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/13) # Start-up menu The start-up menu shows when the user opens the plugin. It allows the user to either load a saved graph or generate a new one. PRs: - [feat: start-up menu](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/10) # Side panel The side panel is a panel of 8 buttons: 1. Move the node left among its siblings. 2. Move the node right among its siblings. 3. Swap the selected person's place with their spouse. 4. Show the current node: the viewpoint will move so the selected node will be in the center of the screen. 5. Return to the start-up menu. 6. Reload persons' data. This button will not update relationships. But it will update a person's data, such as names, images, and dates. 7. Save the current graph. 8. Delete the current graph. # Data directory configuring Now the user can configure the directory that the plugin will scan for people's data. PRs: - [feat: implement dir path configuring;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/20) # Other fixes and small improvements: ## View - [feat(rendering): fixed view during graph re-rendering;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/8) - [feat: move rendering options from model to node data](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/14) - [feat(ui): highlight gender and add buttons for node rearrangement;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/18) - [feat: implement refresh button;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/21) ## Docs - [refactor(layout): doc comments;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/9) - [chore: update readme.md;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/15) - [chore: update `readme.md`](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/17) - [chore(docs): update `readme.md`;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/21) - [chore: v.0.3.0;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/23) ## CI - [ci: add `release.yml` and update `lint.yml`;](https://github.com/TheBestTvarynka/grafily/pull/16)
releases.name: v.0.3.0

releases.author: TheBestTvarynka
releases.description: This is the second release of the Grafily plugin. Now it is closer to what I want, but still far from the end goal. Nevertheless, the list of new features is impressive. ### The V2 of the nodes positioning algorithm Actually, it is not a second version, but rather a complete rewrite with additional features. Now it supports siblings and their spouses' positioning. Even more, it supports rendering sibling' spouse ancestors (parallel family tree). ### Parents collapse Now the user can hide/show persons' descendants. ### Better styling Do not render the unneeded edge handles. Do not render needed buttons. More consistent styling. ## Showcase (I did not have the time and energy to create fake data, so I just blurred sensitive information)
releases.name: v.0.2.0

releases.author: TheBestTvarynka
releases.description:
This is the very first release of the Grafily plugin. The plugin is not even close to being as useful as I want it to be.

This is more like PoC. This initial release includes basic working functionality:

1. Working Obsidian plugin.
2. Persons MD pages parsing and metadata extraction.
3. Family tree rendering using the [`reactflow`](https://reactflow.dev/) library.
4. Implemented [Reingold-Tilford Algorithm](https://towardsdatascience.com/reingold-tilford-algorithm-explained-with-walkthrough-be5810e8ed93/) for calculating tree nodes' positions.

Limitations:

- A person cannot have siblings. It means that the current implementation can only render the parents of each person, not more.
- The plugin can only render the tee. No tree manipulations are implemented.
releases.name: v.0.1.0
